data.title: Evommune, Inc. GAAP EPS of -$11.22, revenue of $13M
data.description:
• Evommune, Inc. press release (EVMN): FY GAAP EPS of -$11.22.
  • Revenue of $13M (+85.7% Y/Y).
  • Cash, cash equivalents and investments were $216.7 million as of December 31, 2025, compared to $72.0 million as of December 31, 2024.

data.title: BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics, Inc. GAAP EPS of -$0.49
data.description:
• BridgeBio Oncology Therapeutics, Inc. press release (BBOT): GAAP EPS of -$0.49.
  • Cash Position: As of December 31, 2025, BBOT had cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities totaling $425.5 million, which is expected to provide cash runway into 2028.
  • Research and development (R&D) expenses: R&D expenses were $38.1 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to $19.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024. The increase in expenses was primarily due to increases in clinical trial expenses and manufacturing expenses for BBO-8520, BBO-11818, and BBO-10203.
  • General and administrative (G&A) expenses: G&A expenses were $5.3 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to $2.3 million for the fourth quarter of 2024. Changes in G&A expenses reflect the initiation of BBOT's standalone operations and de-SPAC transaction.
  • Net Loss: Net loss was $38.8 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to $19.7 million for the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: Marvell in charts: Data center revenue rises nearly 21% Y/Y to $1.65B
Granite Ridge Resources Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 misses by $0.09
data.description:
• Granite Ridge Resources press release (GRNT): Q4
  • Increased total production by 27% to 35,120 Boe/day (49% oil) including a 17% increase in oil production.
  • Reported net loss of $25.1 million, or $(0.19) per share, and Adjusted Net Income (non-GAAP) of $1.5 million, or $0.01 Adjusted Earnings Per Diluted Share (non-GAAP).
  • Generated Adjusted EBITDAX (non-GAAP) of $69.5 million. Invested $127.5 million of capital, placing online 67 gross (10.50 net) wells.
  • Declared a dividend of $0.11 per share.
  • Ended the year with total liquidity of $339.5 million and Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDAX of 1.2x.
  • The Company’s initial 2026 guidance anticipates approximately 34,000 to 36,000 Boe per day of production for 2026, an increase at the midpoint of approximately 9% from 2025.

data.title: Victoria's Secret signals 5%–6% sales growth and margin expansion for 2026 while advancing global and digital strategies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Victoria's Secret & Co. (VSCO) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Hillary Super highlighted, "We returned to growth mode in 2025 with full year comp sales up 5%. Fourth quarter and full year results exceeded top and bottom line guidance, reflecting strength across brands, channels and geographies." She noted that the company reasserted its leadership in bras, reignited PINK, grew the beauty business, and expanded its customer file for the first time in years. Super called out a "signature brand moment" in the 2025 fashion show that "reestablished Victoria's Secret at the center of the cultural conversation and translated directly into business momentum."

  • Super indicated a deliberate evolution in operations, stating, "When we combine great product, powerful storytelling and an elevated experience, our customer responds." She emphasized momentum in bras, panties, and sleepwear, noting, "Victoria's Secret Bra business grew mid-single digits in the fourth quarter," and that sleepwear became "our third largest new customer acquisition category in the quarter."

  • On PINK, Super stated, "For several years, the brand had drifted from its core...In 2025, we reset the foundation and returned PINK to a differentiated position," with high single-digit growth in Q4 and double-digit AUR expansion.

  • Internationally, Super reported, "For the fourth quarter, net sales increased 43% year-over-year with growth across channels and geographies, led by continued strength in China."

  • CFO Scott Sekella stated, "For fiscal 2025, excluding last year's gift card breakage benefit, net sales grew 6% to $6.553 billion. Adjusted operating income rose 16% to $403 million and adjusted EPS increased 22% to $3, all despite $85 million in net tariff pressure."

  • Sekella also addressed portfolio moves, saying, "We have initiated a strategic review of Daily Look...We also continue to assess the Adore Me business and explore opportunities to optimize it within our portfolio."

Outlook

  • Sekella provided guidance: "For fiscal year 2026, we expect net sales to be in the range of $6.85 billion to $6.95 billion compared to net sales of $6.553 billion in fiscal year 2025, representing growth of approximately 5% to 6%."

  • Operating income is expected in the range of $430 million to $460 million, with implied operating margin expansion of approximately 20 to 50 basis points.

  • Fiscal 2026 net income per diluted share is projected to be between $3.20 and $3.45.

  • Sekella stated, "For the first quarter of 2026, we are forecasting net sales in the range of $1.49 billion to $1.525 billion...We estimate first quarter earnings per diluted share to be in the range of $0.20 to $0.30 compared to adjusted earnings per diluted share of $0.09 in the first quarter of 2025."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter net sales were $2.27 billion, an increase of $164 million or 8% over last year.

  • Comp sales increased 8% for the second consecutive quarter. AURs in the quarter were up 6% compared to last year.

  • International sales in Q4 grew 43% to $276 million.

  • Adjusted gross margin dollars were $895 million; adjusted gross margin rate was 39.4%.

  • Adjusted SG&A dollars were $579 million, with an adjusted SG&A rate of 25.5%.

  • Adjusted operating income was $316 million for Q4. Adjusted net income per diluted share was $2.77.

  • Year-end cash balance was $518 million; free cash flow for the year was $312 million (including a $69 million one-time litigation benefit).

Q&A

  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about customer acquisition trends and marketing improvement. Super responded, "We're seeing growth across new, retained and reactivated but the highest growth in new. And within that, we're seeing a nice uptick in younger customers...we are increasing our count of new customers, which feels great."

  • Simeon Siegel, Guggenheim: Inquired about brand size potential and AUR differences. Super explained, "I'm just very optimistic about all 3 business units. I don't see a reason why we cannot hit historical levels of sales in VS&Co in general...we are seeing broad-based success across removing promotions."

  • Mauricio Serna Vega, UBS: Asked about turnaround progress and tariff cadence. Super indicated, "What inning are we in? Early to mid. I think it's different for each business unit...we're in earlier innings [in PINK] because of that." Sekella clarified, "We're assuming tariff rates in place prior to Supreme Court ruling...it will be heavier kind of in the first half and particularly even in the first quarter."

  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory: Asked about PINK stand-alone potential and fashion show learnings. Super described the SoHo pop-up as a laboratory, focusing on traffic, conversion, and store productivity, and noted learnings from the fashion show will shape year-round engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts demonstrated a positive to slightly optimistic tone, with questions focused on sustaining growth, brand positioning, and marketing ROI.

  • Management's tone remained confident in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing "momentum," "confidence," and "runway to growth."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both sides showed increased conviction in the transformation strategy, with less skepticism around market share gains and more discussion of forward opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 net sales and operating income reflects a continued trajectory of growth, with margin expansion and increased capital investment compared to the prior quarter's cautious optimism.

  • Strategic focus has shifted from foundational execution to activating brand heat and leveraging digital and global opportunities.

  • Analysts now probe for upside drivers and long-term scalability, rather than short-term recovery or stabilization.

  • Management's confidence has strengthened, with explicit references to the impact of new leadership and cross-functional execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff headwinds continue, with Sekella stating, "Our fiscal 2026 guidance assumes an incremental gross tariff cost of approximately $160 million...we expect to mitigate most of that impact."

  • The strategic review of Daily Look and restructuring at Adore Me highlight portfolio optimization as an ongoing focus.

  • International risk is present, particularly with store closures in the Middle East, but management noted the royalty-based model limits exposure.

Final Takeaway

Victoria's Secret & Co. delivered a strong close to 2025, with comp sales growth, robust performance across all brands, and a return to market share gains. Management projects continued momentum into 2026, guided by disciplined execution, digital and global expansion, and a focus on new customer acquisition and brand building. The company expects to grow net sales by 5% to 6% and expand operating margins, supported by operational leverage and ongoing mitigation of tariff impacts. Portfolio adjustments and the ability to drive high engagement through brand moments and collaborations remain central to the strategy going forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AI cloud company Together AI, which rents out Nvidia chips, pursues $1B in fresh funding: report
data.description:
Together AI, an artificial intelligence cloud startup that has received backing from Nvidia (NVDA), is in pursuit of $1B in a new funding round, according to The Information.

The San Francisco-based company is currently valued at $7.5B, the report said. Together AI, founded in 2022, is already generating about $1B in annualized revenue, a figure that has nearly tripled in less than one year. The company leases Nvidia chip servers and then rents them out to AI developers. It also buys some servers for its data centers. This new round of funding is mostly for that purpose, according to people familiar with the issue.

The company has raised a total of $554M across five funding rounds, which included investments from notable tech companies such as Nvidia and Salesforce Ventures (CRM).

"To support our rapidly growing ecosystem, we're dramatically expanding our infrastructure," Together AI said. "We've secured 200 MW of power capacity and are deploying optimized clusters of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs across multiple North American data centers. Our recent partnership with Hypertec to co-build a cluster of 36,000 NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 GPUs further strengthens our position as the leading AI Cloud provider."

It is similar to other so-called neoclouds such as Lambda, CoreWeave (CRWV), and Nebius (NBIS).

data.title: Smith & Wesson handgun sales drive FQ3 beat, company turns free cash flow positive
data.description:
Increased sales of handguns helped Smith & Wesson (SWBI) beat Wall Street’s expectations for the fiscal third quarter, with sales gaining more than 17% from a year ago and the company’s profit margin expanding by 210 basis points.

"We were very pleased with our third quarter results, which demonstrated continued market share growth—while simultaneously maintaining resiliency in our pricing power and profitability,” said CEO Mark Smith, noting the 28% increase in handgun shipments to its sporting goods channel.

Efforts to trim inventory over the past twelve months now enables the firearm manufacturers to focus on increasing production to meet market demand and drive up margins. As a result, Smith & Wesson (SWBI) expects fourth-quarter sales to increase 10% to 12% from the prior year’s fourth quarter.

For the fiscal third quarter, SWBI earned an adjusted profit of $0.08 per share, up from $0.03 per share a year ago and $0.03 better than expected. Taking out recurring charges, the company realized a profit of $16.8M, or 12.4% of net sales, compared with $13.9M, or 12% of net sales, for the same quarter last year. 

Net sales were up 17.1% to $135.71M, beating expectations by more than $10M.

On the balance sheet, the company generated $16.8M in free cash flow, reversing from negative $16.15M a year ago, as operating cash flow improved to $20.45M from negative $9.8M in the fiscal third quarter of 2025.

Smith & Wesson (SWBI) shares were up as much as 13% in after-hours trading, giving a boost to Sturm, Ruger & Company (RGR) and Outdoor Holding Company (POWW).

data.title: Lexicon outlines 2026 milestones as SONATA-HCM enrollment surpasses 50% and cash position is strengthened
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (LXRX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Michael Exton, CEO, highlighted, "Lexicon is advancing 3 very strong novel late-stage programs in the therapeutic areas of cardiometabolic disease and chronic pain." He emphasized progress in sotagliflozin development for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM), plans for an NDA submission in type 1 diabetes, and a partnership with Viatris for markets outside the U.S. and Europe. The company is also advancing LX9851 in obesity with Novo Nordisk and pilavapadin for diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain (DPNP), for which it seeks a development partner. Exton stated, "We've also been diligent about driving operational excellence as well as improving our financial position and cost structure to sustainably support our core programs going forward."
  • Exton further noted, "The SONATA-HCM Phase III trial of SOTA for obstructive and nonobstructive HCM is enrolling well, and we expect to complete enrollment in the middle of this year." He outlined expectations to resubmit the NDA for Zynquista in 2026 with potential approval later that year, and underscored the bolstered financial position after a recent capital raise and a Novo Nordisk milestone payment.
  • CFO Scott Coiante reported, "Total revenues were $5.5 million and $49.8 million for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025, respectively." He added, "Research and development expenses for the fourth quarter of 2025 decreased to $11.3 million from $26.7 million in 2024."
  • Coiante elaborated, "We also meaningfully improved our cost structure for 2025 with operating expenses down $129.5 million for 2025 as compared to '24 reflecting our strategic repositioning in late 2024 and substantially reduced marketing and promotional spend for INPEFA in 2025."

Outlook

  • Coiante provided 2026 guidance: "We expect total operating expenses to be between $100 million and $110 million. R&D expenses are expected to be between $63 million and $68 million and do not include costs associated with Phase III pivotal studies of pilavapadin as our goal would be to move this asset forward with a development partner. SG&A expenses...are expected to range between $37 million and $42 million."
  • Management expects to complete SONATA-HCM enrollment by mid-2026 and resubmit the NDA for Zynquista in 2026, with a potential approval before year-end.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenues were $5.5 million, including $4.3 million from the Novo Nordisk agreement and $1.1 million in net sales of INPEFA. Full year revenues totaled $49.8 million, with $45 million from Novo Nordisk and $4.6 million in INPEFA net sales.
  • Net loss for Q4 2025 was ($15.5 million) or ($0.04) per share, improving from ($33.8 million) or ($0.09) per share in Q4 2024. Full year net loss was ($50.3 million) or ($0.14) per share, compared to ($200.4 million) or ($0.63) per share the prior year.
  • As of December 31, 2025, cash, investments, and restricted cash stood at $125.2 million, with the company subsequently raising more than $100 million after year-end.
  • Operating expenses were reduced by $39 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to the prior year and by $129.5 million for the full year, reflecting strategic repositioning and reduced marketing spend.
  • Total debt was reduced by approximately $46.3 million in 2025.

Q&A

  • Matthew Barcus, Jefferies: Asked about STENO1 study data sufficiency for Zynquista approval and review timelines. Exton responded, "We're expecting a 6-month review here. So reemphasizing that the data that we're seeing, we expect a submission this year and as well an approval before the end of 2026." Granowitz added details on DKA rates, confirming alignment with FDA requirements.
  • Yigal Nochomovitz, Citigroup: Inquired about pilavapadin partnership progress and Novo's plans for LX9851. Exton indicated ongoing dialogue with partners and highlighted increased specificity in discussions following regulatory feedback. Regarding LX9851, Exton emphasized Novo's strategic pivot toward oral obesity treatments, while Granowitz described the complementary mechanism to semaglutide.
  • Joseph Pantginis, H.C. Wainwright: Questioned pilavapadin development flexibility and the impact of supporting studies for SOTA in HCM. Exton clarified the company is not planning to start Phase III on its own, prioritizing investment in T1D and HCM. Granowitz explained the supportive nature of the SOTA-CARDIA and SOTA-CROSS studies for future sNDAs.
  • Shannon (Piper Sandler): Asked about SONATA-HCM enrollment pace. Granowitz affirmed enrollment is ahead of projections, with both obstructive and nonobstructive cohorts progressing, and Exton stated confidence in achieving midyear enrollment targets.
  • Byunghyun Ahn, Leerink Partners: Sought clarification on pilavapadin Phase III enrollment criteria. Granowitz noted expanded eligibility due to a renal impairment study and possible changes to the minimum neuropathy duration requirement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on regulatory timelines, partnership clarity, and trial progress, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone and seeking confirmation of catalysts. There was an emphasis on timing and risk mitigation.
  • Management presented a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently emphasizing operational discipline and program momentum. Exton stated, "We have the pipeline. We have the team, and we have the momentum, and I'm incredibly excited about what lies ahead."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management's tone has shifted from cautious progress updates to highlighting tangible achievements and forward momentum, while analysts remained consistently focused on execution risks and milestone deliverability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 operating expenses was narrowed to $100–110 million, from $105–115 million previously. R&D expense expectations shifted lower, while SG&A guidance increased slightly. The company reported a strengthened cash position following additional capital raises, compared to $145 million in cash at Q3 end.
  • Strategic focus continues on advancing late-stage assets in cardiometabolic and pain indications, but the narrative now includes clear timing for SONATA-HCM enrollment completion and NDA resubmission for Zynquista.
  • Analysts' questions remain centered on regulatory and partnership timelines, though with greater emphasis on near-term catalysts and financial flexibility versus prior quarter's focus on strategic reorientation and foundational progress.
  • Management's confidence and communication of operational discipline have grown, with a more robust discussion of financial improvements and capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted reliance on successful clinical trial enrollment and regulatory review timelines for key programs, especially SONATA-HCM and Zynquista.
  • The company is dependent on partnership execution for pilavapadin's advancement into Phase III, and delays in finding a suitable partner could affect timelines.
  • Analyst questions surfaced concerns regarding the sufficiency of safety data for Zynquista, the cadence and balance of SONATA enrollment, and the specifics of Phase III design for pilavapadin.
  • Ongoing cost management and operational discipline are being prioritized to mitigate financial risks, with no new concerns about liquidity following the recent capital raise.

Final Takeaway

Lexicon Pharmaceuticals enters 2026 with significant momentum across its core programs, supported by a strengthened financial position and operational discipline. The company expects to complete SONATA-HCM enrollment by midyear, targets NDA resubmission and potential approval for Zynquista within the year, and remains focused on securing a partner for pilavapadin while advancing LX9851 in collaboration with Novo Nordisk. Management expresses high confidence in delivering on these milestones, viewing the coming year as pivotal for potential value creation and strategic progress.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Puranium Energy announces proposed non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Puranium Energy (CSE: UX) on Thursday said that it has a non-brokered private placement of up to 7,252,781 units at a price of $0.08 per unit for gross proceeds of $580,223. 
  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share and one common share purchase warrant, with each warrant exercisable at $0.125 for a period of thirty-six months from the closing of the offering.
  • All securities issued pursuant to the offering will be subject to a four-month-and-one-day statutory hold period and legended accordingly.
  • No finders fees will be paid in connection with the offering.
  • Proceeds will be used toward general working capital, including about $185,000 to settle creditor obligations.
  • MREYD closed +31.14% at $0.08.
  • Source: Press Release
